sections.markdown:
# Palo Alto Firewall Log Analysis

This notebook contains DQL queries for analyzing Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS traffic logs ingested by the **Palo Alto Firewall Log Generator** Dynatrace workflow.

The workflow generates a realistic mix of **~60% allowed** and **~40% blocked** traffic across multiple applications, network zones, and firewall rules. Logs are ingested into Grail with structured `paloalto.*` attributes.

---

## 1. All Palo Alto Firewall Logs — Overview
Shows all ingested firewall log entries with key parsed fields.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "palo-alto-firewall"
| fields timestamp,
         paloalto.action,
         paloalto.src,
         paloalto.dst,
         paloalto.app,
         paloalto.rule,
         paloalto.from_zone,
         paloalto.to_zone,
         paloalto.dport,
         paloalto.bytes_sent,
         paloalto.bytes_received,
         paloalto.session_end_reason,
         paloalto.device_name,
         content
| sort timestamp desc
| limit 100

sections.markdown:
## 2. Blocked Traffic Only
All denied, dropped, and reset sessions — validates the firewall **IS** blocking traffic.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "palo-alto-firewall"
| filter paloalto.action != "allow"
| fields timestamp,
         paloalto.action,
         paloalto.src,
         paloalto.dst,
         paloalto.app,
         paloalto.rule,
         paloalto.dport,
         paloalto.from_zone,
         paloalto.to_zone,
         paloalto.session_end_reason
| sort timestamp desc
| limit 100

sections.markdown:
## 3. Allowed Traffic Only
All permitted sessions — validates the firewall is **NOT** blocking all traffic.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "palo-alto-firewall"
| filter paloalto.action == "allow"
| fields timestamp,
         paloalto.action,
         paloalto.src,
         paloalto.dst,
         paloalto.app,
         paloalto.rule,
         paloalto.dport,
         paloalto.bytes_sent,
         paloalto.bytes_received
| sort timestamp desc
| limit 100

sections.markdown:
## 4. Traffic Summary — Allow vs Block
Single-glance validation: is the expected mix of allowed and blocked traffic present?

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "palo-alto-firewall"
| fieldsAdd is_blocked = paloalto.action != "allow"
| summarize total = count(),
            allowed = countIf(NOT is_blocked),
            blocked = countIf(is_blocked)
| fieldsAdd allowed_pct = round(toDouble(allowed) / toDouble(total) * 100, decimals: 1),
            blocked_pct = round(toDouble(blocked) / toDouble(total) * 100, decimals: 1)

sections.markdown:
## 5. Blocked Traffic by Firewall Rule
Which deny rules are firing most frequently?

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "palo-alto-firewall"
| filter paloalto.action != "allow"
| summarize block_count = count(),
            unique_sources = countDistinct(paloalto.src),
            unique_apps = countDistinct(paloalto.app),
            by: { paloalto.rule }
| sort block_count desc

sections.markdown:
## 6. Blocked Traffic by Application
Which applications are being denied by the firewall?

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "palo-alto-firewall"
| filter paloalto.action != "allow"
| summarize block_count = count(),
            deny_actions = collectDistinct(paloalto.action),
            rules_triggered = collectDistinct(paloalto.rule),
            by: { paloalto.app }
| sort block_count desc

sections.markdown:
## 7. Blocked Traffic by Action Type
Breakdown of deny vs drop vs reset-both actions.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "palo-alto-firewall"
| filter paloalto.action != "allow"
| summarize count = count(),
            by: { paloalto.action }
| sort count desc

sections.markdown:
## 8. Zone Pair Analysis
Traffic flow between network zones — useful to see inbound vs outbound blocking patterns.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "palo-alto-firewall"
| summarize total = count(),
            allowed = countIf(paloalto.action == "allow"),
            blocked = countIf(paloalto.action != "allow"),
            by: { paloalto.from_zone, paloalto.to_zone }
| fieldsAdd block_rate = round(toDouble(blocked) / toDouble(total) * 100, decimals: 1)
| sort block_rate desc

sections.markdown:
## 9. Top Blocked Destinations
Which internal IPs are being targeted most by blocked traffic?

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "palo-alto-firewall"
| filter paloalto.action != "allow"
| summarize block_count = count(),
            attacking_sources = countDistinct(paloalto.src),
            apps_targeted = collectDistinct(paloalto.app),
            ports_targeted = collectDistinct(paloalto.dport),
            by: { paloalto.dst }
| sort block_count desc
| limit 20

sections.markdown:
## 10. Top Blocked Sources
Which external IPs are being denied most frequently?

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "palo-alto-firewall"
| filter paloalto.action != "allow"
| summarize block_count = count(),
            targets = countDistinct(paloalto.dst),
            rules_hit = collectDistinct(paloalto.rule),
            by: { paloalto.src }
| sort block_count desc
| limit 20

sections.markdown:
## 11. Blocked Traffic Over Time
Time series showing blocked vs allowed traffic trend.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "palo-alto-firewall"
| fieldsAdd is_blocked = paloalto.action != "allow"
| makeTimeseries allowed = countIf(NOT is_blocked),
                 blocked = countIf(is_blocked),
                 interval: 5m

sections.markdown:
## 12. High-Risk Port Blocks
Traffic blocked on sensitive ports: RDP (3389), SQL (1433/3306), FTP (21), suspicious (4444).

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "palo-alto-firewall"
| filter paloalto.action != "allow"
| filter paloalto.dport == 3389
     OR paloalto.dport == 1433
     OR paloalto.dport == 3306
     OR paloalto.dport == 21
     OR paloalto.dport == 4444
| fields timestamp,
         paloalto.src,
         paloalto.dst,
         paloalto.dport,
         paloalto.app,
         paloalto.action,
         paloalto.rule,
         paloalto.session_end_reason
| sort timestamp desc

sections.markdown:
## 13. Session End Reason Breakdown
Why are sessions ending? `policy-deny` and `threat` indicate blocks; `tcp-fin`/`tcp-rst` are normal closures.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "palo-alto-firewall"
| summarize count = count(),
            by: { paloalto.session_end_reason, paloalto.action }
| sort count desc

sections.markdown:
## 14. Bandwidth Analysis
Bytes transferred by allowed connections grouped by application. Blocked sessions transfer 0 bytes.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "palo-alto-firewall"
| filter paloalto.action == "allow"
| summarize total_bytes_sent = sum(paloalto.bytes_sent),
            total_bytes_received = sum(paloalto.bytes_received),
            session_count = count(),
            by: { paloalto.app }
| fieldsAdd total_bytes = total_bytes_sent + total_bytes_received
| sort total_bytes desc

sections.markdown:
## 15. Raw CSV Log Parsing (Fallback)
Parses fields directly from the raw CSV content using the Dynatrace Pattern Language. Use this if logs were ingested without the structured `paloalto.*` attributes.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "palo-alto-firewall"
| parse content, "
    LD*:future1 ','
    LD:receive_time ','
    LD:serial ','
    LD:type ','
    LD:subtype ','
    LD*:future2 ','
    LD:gen_time ','
    LD:src_ip ','
    LD:dst_ip ','
    LD:nat_src ','
    LD:nat_dst ','
    LD:rule ','
    LD*:src_user ','
    LD*:dst_user ','
    LD:app ','
    LD:vsys ','
    LD:from_zone ','
    LD:to_zone ','
    LD:inbound_if ','
    LD:outbound_if ','
    LD:logset ','
    LD*:future3 ','
    LD:session_id ','
    LD:repeat_cnt ','
    LD:src_port ','
    LD:dst_port ','
    LD:nat_src_port ','
    LD:nat_dst_port ','
    LD:flags ','
    LD:protocol ','
    LD:action ','
    LD:total_bytes ','
    LD:bytes_sent ','
    LD:bytes_recv ','
    LD:packets ','
    LD:start_time ','
    LD:elapsed ','
    LD:category
  "
| fields timestamp, action, src_ip, dst_ip, app, rule, from_zone, to_zone,
         dst_port, bytes_sent, bytes_recv, category
| sort timestamp desc
| limit 50
